steps.title: Find the introductory phrase
steps.content: The sentence begins with an introductory prepositional phrase: With almost two millennia of climate data in its tree rings. A comma should separate that phrase from the main clause.

steps.title: Reject bad boundaries
steps.content: Choice A incorrectly uses and to connect the phrase to the main clause. Choice C turns the introductory phrase into a fragment. Choice D uses a semicolon even though the first part is not an independent clause.

steps.title: Choose the comma
steps.content: A comma after rings correctly marks the boundary before the main clause, a single tree like this can tell the history of the world. The answer is B.
